name: Mendaur Circular Trail
description: This difficult 6.6-mile trail run from Aurtitz offers 2889 feet of elevation gain and panoramic views from Mendaur summit.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Ituren are set within the picturesque Pyrenean foothills of northern Navarra, offering a naturally varied and often challenging terrain. The region is characterized by verdant fields, significant inclines, and diverse paths, providing a compelling natural setting for outdoor activities. Proximity to Bertiz Lordship Natural Park and Embalse de Leurtza Urtegia further enhances the opportunities for scenic running amidst natural features. This mountainous landscape offers varied surfaces beneficial for engaging different muscle groups and improving balance. Best jogging routes around Ituren The most popular jogging route is Mendaur Circular Trail, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the mountainous landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Mendaur loop from Arantza, a difficult 8.5 miles (13.7 km) path. This route offers sustained climbs and panoramic views of the surrounding Pyrenean foothills. Local runners also love the Mendaur Summit and Hermitage loop from Ituren, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with substantial ascents, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Jogging around Ituren is defined by Pyrenean foothills, verdant fields, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature significant elevation changes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 90 runners have used komoot to explore Ituren's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of running routes can I find around Ituren?
A: Ituren, nestled in the Pyrenean foothills of northern Navarra, offers a variety of running routes characterized by naturally varied and often challenging terrain. You'll encounter significant inclines, diverse paths, and verdant fields, providing a compelling natural setting for outdoor activities. The mountainous landscape ensures varied surfaces, which are beneficial for engaging different muscle groups and improving balance.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Ituren area?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Ituren, offering a range of experiences for different preferences. The area is particularly known for its more challenging trails, with the majority featuring significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Ituren?
A: The running routes around Ituren are predominantly challenging, with most featuring significant elevation gain due to the Pyrenean foothills terrain. While there is one moderate route, the area is best suited for runners comfortable with ascents and varied surfaces. Beginners might find the trails demanding but rewarding for building strength.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running near Ituren?
A: While running near Ituren, you'll be immersed in stunning Pyrenean foothills and verdant scenery. You might encounter natural attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5840990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bisusto Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy panoramic views from high points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1098435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendaur Summit and Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;. The region's proximity to Bertiz Lordship Natural Park also suggests opportunities for scenic runs amidst diverse natural environments.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Ituren?
A: Yes, several popular running routes around Ituren are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971576790/mendaur-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendaur Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 6.6-mile (10.6 km) loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31919497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendaur Summit and Hermitage loop from Ituren&lt;/a&gt; offers an 8.0-mile (12.9 km) experience through varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for trail running in Ituren?
A: The Pyrenean foothills around Ituren offer beautiful running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm but is still suitable, especially in the mornings or evenings. Winter running is possible, but conditions may vary, with potential for colder weather and occasional snow at higher elevations.

Q: Can I find running routes that offer views of the Embalse de Leurtza Urtegia?
A: Yes, the Embalse de Leurtza Urtegia is a natural scenic spot near Ituren that likely offers peaceful and picturesque trails around the reservoir. While specific routes are not detailed in this guide, its proximity suggests opportunities for tranquil runs with beautiful water views.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Ituren?
A: The running trails around Ituren are highly regarded by the komoot community, with over 90 runners having explored the varied terrain. Users often praise the challenging nature of the routes, the stunning Pyrenean landscapes, and the rewarding panoramic views from summits like Mendaur.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options around Ituren?
A: Yes, Ituren offers several longer running routes for those seeking a more extended challenge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31930978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendaur loop from Ituren&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 9.8 miles (15.7 km) with significant elevation, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40340743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendaur Summit and Hermitage – Mendaur loop from Sunbilla&lt;/a&gt; is about 9.0 miles (14.5 km), both offering sustained climbs and expansive views.

Q: Is public transport available to access the running trails near Ituren?
A: While Ituren is a picturesque village, specific public transport links directly to all trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local transport options or consider driving to access some of the more remote starting points for the running routes in the Pyrenean foothills.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass through or near the Bertiz Lordship Natural Park?
A: The Bertiz Lordship Natural Park is located just 7.6 km from Ituren and is a significant attraction for outdoor enthusiasts. While this guide doesn't detail specific running routes within the park, its extensive network of trails and paths makes it an excellent area to explore for additional running opportunities near Ituren.
tips:
With its top at 1131 meters above sea level. the Mendaur is a real challenge!
Located in the Malerreka valley, in the pre-Pyrenees, it offers a unique and incomparable landscape with beautiful views of the towns of Sunbilla and Ituren.

faqs.question: How challenging is the trail run from Aurtitz?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as difficult for trail running, requiring a very good fitness level. The route involves significant elevation changes, with an ascent of approximately 880 meters, and varied terrain that demands good footing.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: You will encounter a varied and demanding terrain. It starts with a gravel path, transitions to a wide track, and then meanders through a typical Navarrese forest. Expect stream crossings, small waterfalls, and a steep ascent towards the Mendaur Pass and summit. The path can be uneven in forest sections, so sturdy shoes are essential.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a magnificent journey through a typical Navarrese forest. As you ascend, you'll enjoy increasingly expansive views, culminating in breathtaking 360° panoramic views from the summit of Mendaur. A visit to the Ermita de la Trinidad (Hermitage of the Trinity) at the peak is also a notable point of interest.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: While the research doesn't specify an exact best time, considering the forest sections and potential for stream crossings, spring and autumn would likely offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer could be warm, and winter might present snow or ice at higher elevations, especially near Mendaur.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Aurtitz. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's generally advisable to look for designated parking areas within or on the outskirts of Aurtitz village, or consult local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the trail run from Aurtitz dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed or if there are specific leash rules. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access this trail. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as 'difficult' for trail running and requires a very good fitness level due to its significant elevation gain and varied, often challenging, terrain. Beginners might find it overly strenuous.

faqs.question: What makes this trail run particularly notable?
faqs.answer: This trail is notable for its spectacular 360-degree panoramic views from the summit of Mendaur, the cultural interest of the Ermita de la Trinidad at the peak, and the immersive experience within a magnificent typical Navarrese forest. It offers a significant physical challenge for experienced trail runners.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of official trails such as Bustitza - Uztakorta, Lakaingo lepoa - Bustitza, Lesaka-Bustitza, and Sagartxarko ubidearen bidea.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a trail run on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied and demanding terrain, including uneven forest paths and stream crossings, you should bring sturdy trail running shoes with good grip. Additionally, carry enough water, snacks, appropriate layers for changing weather conditions, and navigation tools.
